description_long: This is a rustic wooden barn with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in weathered shingles. The structure is supported by visible timber posts along the sides, giving it a sturdy, traditional appearance. The front features a large, open entryway with a hinged wooden door that can be propped open, while the rear is fully enclosed with horizontal wooden planks. The sides of the barn are clad in vertical wooden boards, and there are no visible windows on any side. The overall design is simple and utilitarian, typical of agricultural outbuildings found in rural or peri-urban settings. The barn's construction suggests it is intended for storage or sheltering livestock, with ample interior space accessible through the main entrance.
description: A rustic wooden barn with a steep gable roof, timber supports, and a large open entryway. The exterior features weathered wood siding and no visible windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large open entryway and the pitched gable roof, with the interior partially visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the weathered shingle roof, vertical timber supports, and the solid wooden siding with no windows.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the fully enclosed back wall with horizontal wooden planks and no openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the roof, timber supports, and solid siding.
attribute_door_style: single large hinged door, propped open
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any side
